Filter.description:
OData Version 4.0 filter string (limited functionality). Supports only 'and' conjunction ('or' and 'not' are NOT supported). Supported fields and operators are in the below given table.
| Field | Operators |
| --------- | -------- |
| `siteId` | `eq` and `in` |
| `siteName` | `eq` and `in` |
| `serialNumber` | `eq` and `in` |
| `deviceName` | `eq` and `in` |
| `deviceType` | `eq` and `in` |
| `upgradeStatus` | `eq` and `in` |
| `firmwareClassification` | `eq` and `in` |
| `firmwareVersion` | `eq` and `in` |

**The operators are implemented as follows:**

* `eq`: Only values exactly matching the given value are returned.

* `in`: Only values present in the given list are returned.
Filter.name: filter

Id.description: Unique identifier for the webhook. This can be obtained from the List Webhooks API.
Id.name: id

SortV1.description:
Comma separated list of sort expressions. Each sort expression is a property name optionally followed by a direction indicator asc(ascending) or desc (descending).
The first sort expression in the list defines the primary sort order, the second defines the secondary sort order, and so on. If a direction indicator is omitted the default direction is ascending.
Supported fields are `siteId`, `serialNumber`, `deviceName`, `deviceType`, `upgradeStatus`, `firmwareClassification`, and `firmwareVersion`.
SortV1.name: sort

Accuracy.description: Indicates the radius of horizontal uncertainty, computed at 95%. This means the sum of the probability of potential locations contained in this uncertainty circle represents 95% of the whole venue probability. The unit of this radius is in metres.

Action.description: Brief text specifying the executed operation.

elevation.description: The elevation in meters in relation to the vertical datum identified by the "elevationType" field, with negative values indicating elevations below it.

elevationType.description:
Identifies the vertical datum used to measure the device elevation.
* 'ABOVE_MEAN_SEA_LEVEL' - Above Mean Sea Level, the elevation in meters in relation to the zero set by the vertical WGS 84 datum
* 'ABOVE_GROUND_LEVEL'  - Above Ground Level, the elevation in meters above the local ground level
* 'UNKNOWN' - The elevation type is unknown

elevationUncertainty.description:
The elevation uncertainty in meters above and below the value specified in the "elevation" field.

The value 0 is reserved to indicate that the elevation uncertainty is unknown.

DeviceLocationSummary.description:
Geographic coordinates of a device.

**consolidatedLocation** contains the best estimate of the device location.  The location in this field can be derived automatically by the system or overridden manually by the system administrator (by placing it on a floorplan or via another NBAPI call). The sytem generated location is almost always available, when an AP is removed from a floorplan then the system generated location will be used as the consolidated location.

Supported filter fields are: floorId and buildingId.

FloorLevel.description:
If included this field contains a logical representation of the floor level. This value is relevant only in relation to a building; the value is relative to the ground level of the building which is assigned the number 0.

Determining what floor is at ground level and what constitutes a sub-floor as opposed to a naturally numbered floor is left to local interpretation.

Larger values represent floors that are farther away from floor 0 such that
* if positive the floor value is farther above the ground floor
* if negative the floor value is farther below the ground floor

Non-integer values can be used to represent intermediate or sub-floors, such as mezzanine levels.  Example: a mezzanine between floor 1 and floor 2 could be represented as a value of 1.25.  Example: mezzanines between floor 4 and floor 5 could be represented as values of 4.5 and 4.75.

If the field is absent floor 0 must be assumed.

IpAddress.description: Source IP Address of the user executed the operation.

LCIUncertainty.description:
Uncertainty ellipse centered on the latitude and longitude of the "center" field of the parent object.

The axes of this ellipse are parallel to north-south and east-west directions.

confidenceLevel.description:
Represents the probability that the location of the device lies within the area of the ellipse.

The value 0 is reserved to indicate that the confidence level is unknown.

latitudeUncertainty.description:
The latitude uncertainty in degrees.

The latitudeUncertainty is added to the latitude value in the center field of the associated Location to determine the maximum value in the uncertainty range; similarly, the uncertainty is subtracted from the base value to determine the minimum value.

If the maximum or minimum latitude values derived from applying uncertainty are outside the range of -90 to +90 then these values are trimmed to within this range.

The value 0 is reserved to indicate that the latitude uncertainty is unknown.

longitudeUncertainty.description:
The longitude uncertainty in degrees.

The longitudeUncertainty is added to the base longitude value in the center field of the associated Location to determine the maximum value in the uncertainty range; similarly, the uncertainty is subtracted from the base value to determine the minimum value.

If the maximum or minimum longitude values derived from applying uncertainty are outside the range of -180 to +180 then these values are normalized to this range by adding or subtracting 360 as necessary.

The value 0 is reserved to indicate that the longitude uncertainty is unknown.

passerbyCount.description: The number of passerby for the site in the given time range.

Latitude.description:
Latitude of the device location in decimal degrees with 6 decimal places of precision.

The allowed range is from -90 to +90. Positive values represent latitudes north of the equator; negative values south of the equator.

The value shall be relative to the WGS 84 datum.

Latitudeasset.description:
Latitude of the Asset Tag location in decimal degrees with 6 decimal places of precision.

The allowed range is from -90 to +90. Positive values represent latitudes north of the equator; negative values south of the equator.

The value shall be relative to the WGS 84 datum.

Latitudewifi.description:
Latitude of the client location in decimal degrees with 6 decimal places of precision.

The allowed range is from -90 to +90. Positive values represent latitudes north of the equator; negative values south of the equator.

The value shall be relative to the WGS 84 datum.

Location.description:
Longitude, latitude, altitude and floor position.

The system can return location uncertainty in two formats.

The **LCIUncertainty** field is a similar ellipse but with a fixed orientation such that its axes are parallel to the north-south and east-west geographic directions.

The system will return both uncertainty representations if they are available.  Note that for a given data set the uncertaintyEllipse will always describe an area equal to or smaller than the LCIUncertainty.

source.description:
Source of the device coordinates.
* 'ADMIN_SPECIFIED' - The device location was administratively entered.
* 'SYSTEM_GENERATED' - The location automatically determined by the system.

Longitude.description:
Longitude of the device location in decimal degrees with 6 decimal places of precision.

The allowed range is from -180 to +180. Positive values represent longitudes east of the prime meridian; negative values west of the prime meridian.

The value shall be relative to the WGS 84 datum.

Longitudeasset.description:
Longitude of the Asset Tag location in decimal degrees with 6 decimal places of precision.

The allowed range is from -180 to +180. Positive values represent longitudes east of the prime meridian; negative values west of the prime meridian.

The value shall be relative to the WGS 84 datum.

Longitudewifi.description:
Longitude of the client location in decimal degrees with 6 decimal places of precision.

The allowed range is from -180 to +180. Positive values represent longitudes east of the prime meridian; negative values west of the prime meridian.

The value shall be relative to the WGS 84 datum.

ScanStatus.description:
The current scan state.

PENDING - Scan request was accepted and awaiting scheduling by the system.
ABORTED - Scan request was delayed and could not be scheduled before the calculated start time
CANCELLED - Scan request was cancelled by the user
SCHEDULED - Scan request has been scheduled by the system and is awaiting the scan start time
SCANNING - Scan is currently running (wifi network connectivity is currently unavailable)
COMPLETE - Scan has completed

StartApRangingScanResponse.description: The result for the ftm scan start request.

errorMessage.description: Error details in the case that a scan could not be started.

result.description:
The start scan request outcome.
SUCCESS - Scan was successfully created and is now PENDING
SCAN_ALREADY_ACTIVE - Scan already active on the given floor-id. Only one scan is allowed per floor at any given time. Scan cannot be started.
NOT_ENOUGH_APS - There were not enough ftm-monitor enabled APs on the given floor-id. Scan cannot be started.
REGULATORY_DOMAIN_MISMATCH - Not all APs are configured with the same country code. Scan cannot be started.
NO_VALID_CHANNELS - Based on the user configured country code and channel selections, there are no available channels to run the ftm scan on. Scan cannot be started.
ERROR - There was an error starting the scan (see errorMessage for details of the error).

post.description:
(USE WITH CAUTION) Will cause a wifi network outage! 

Starts an AP ranging scan for the given floor-id. 

AP ranging scans facilitate using automatic AP placement within the floorplan manager as it allows the system to collect inter-AP ranging information in a much shorter time frame.

During an AP ranging scan, all wifi client connectivity on the 5GHz radio will be disrupted for the system-calculated scan duration. When the scan completes, the network will automatically restore itself.

Only one scan can be active on a floor at any time.

The other pre-requisites for starting an AP ranging scan are:

1. APs on the given floor-id must be 'ftm-monitor' capable (ie. Running AOS firmware version 10.7.1 or greater)
2. APs on the given floor-id must be configured in the same regulatory domain (ie. configured with the same country code)
3. APs on the given floor must be synchronized with the same ntp server

parameters.description: The Site ID where the scan(s) were executed.
parameters.name: site-id

parameters.description: The Floor ID where the scan(s) were executed.
parameters.name: floor-id

parameters.description: If present, the scan will not be started, but the request will be validated.
parameters.name: dry-run

scanStartTime.description: Timestamp in milliseconds since epoch UTC indicating the start time of the ftm-monitor mode scan. All APs will stop servicing wifi clients on the 5GHz radio 2 minutes before this time and will start the scan at the specified time. The minimum value is 5 minutes from the current time, the default value is 10 minutes from the current time if this parameter is not provided.

post.summary: Start an AP ranging scan (USE WITH CAUTION)

get.description: This API endpoint retrieves a list of Asset Tags. Each Asset Tag in the returned list includes metadata details and last known location. The query parameters allow you to narrow down the results to access points that meet specific criteria, ensuring you receive only the relevant data.

parameters.description: OData 4.0 filter (limited functionality). Supports filtering on field `metadata/labels` only.
parameters.name: filter

parameters.description:
MAC address of the Asset Tag.

Note: The filter, sort, limit, and offset parameters are not applicable if the MAC-address query parameter is specified.
parameters.name: mac-address

parameters.description: Retrieve data starting at the specified timestamp. Provided in RFC 3339 format. Example: `2023-01-01T23:10:41.123Z`. If no timestamp is provided, all data will be considered.
parameters.name: start-at

parameters.description: Denotes the maximum number of Asset Tags returned in the response. (Default: 100)
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Specifies the zero-based resource offset from which to start the page. (Default: 0)
parameters.name: offset

parameters.description:
Comma-separated list of sort expressions. Each sort expression is a property name optionally followed by a direction indicator: asc (ascending) or desc (descending).

The first sort expression in the list defines the primary sort order, the second defines the secondary sort order, and so on. If a direction indicator is omitted, the default direction is ascending.

Supported fields are `metadata/name`, `macAddress`, `lastKnownLocation/batteryLevel`, and `lastKnownLocation/lastSeen`.
parameters.name: sort

get.summary: Get a list of Asset Tags

get.description: This API retrieves detailed information about a specific Asset Tag.

parameters.description: ID of the Asset Tag
parameters.name: asset-tag-id

get.summary: Get Asset Tag details

delete.description: This API removes all metadata parameters for a specific Asset Tag.

parameters.description: ID of the Asset Tag
parameters.name: asset-tag-id

202.description: Asset Tag metadata delete request has been accepted

post.description: This API creates metadata for a specific Asset Tag.

parameters.description: ID of the Asset Tag
parameters.name: asset-tag-id

schema.description: Object with request parameters of Asset Tag metadata

202.description: Asset Tag metadata update request has been accepted

put.description: This API replaces Asset Tag metadata with the request body content.

parameters.description: ID of the Asset Tag
parameters.name: asset-tag-id

schema.description: Object with request parameters of Asset Tag metadata

202.description: Asset Tag metadata update request has been accepted

get.description: This API retrieves all the audit logs based on the tenants.

parameters.description: Data is required starting from this timestamp, provided in RFC 3339 (and ISO 8601) format in the UTC timezone with milliseconds. The difference between *end-at* and *start-at* should be less than 90 days.
parameters.name: start-at

parameters.description: Data is required up to this timestamp, provided in RFC 3339 (and ISO 8601) format in the UTC timezone with milliseconds. The timestamp must be earlier than the current time and later than the start-at.
parameters.name: end-at

parameters.description:
OData Version 4.0 filter string (limited functionality). Supports only 'and' conjunction ('or' and 'not' are NOT supported). Supported fields and operators are in the below given table.

| Field | Operators |
| --------- | -------- |
| `action` | `eq` and `in` |
| `description` | `eq` and `in` |
| `destination` | `eq` and `in` |
| `category` | `eq` and `in` |
| `subCategory` | `eq` and `in` |
| `destinationName` | `eq` and `in` |
| `ipAddress` | `eq` and `in` |
| `source` | `eq` and `in` |

**The operators are implemented as follows:**

* `eq`: Only values exactly matching the given value are returned.

* `in`: Only values present in the given list are returned.

.
parameters.name: filter

schema.example: action eq 'Scope mapped' and  category eq 'Alert Management' and destination eq 'Global'

parameters.description:
Comma separated list of sort expressions. Each sort expression is a property name optionally followed by a direction indicator asc (ascending) or desc (descending).

The first sort expression in the list defines the primary sort order, the second defines the secondary sort order, and so on. If a direction indicator is omitted the default direction is ascending.

Supported fields are port, speed, neighbour, neighbourFamily, nativeVlan, neighbourRole, alias, connector, lag, vlanMode, poeClass, ipv4 and mtu. Default sort by name.
parameters.name: sort

parameters.description: Specifies the maximum number of Audits returned in the response list.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Number of items to skip before returning results.
parameters.name: offset

get.summary: Get audit logs belonging to the tenant.

get.description: Retrieves the audit log entry for a given audit ID.

parameters.description: Audit Id log required
parameters.name: id

get.summary: Get Audit log based on the audit id.

get.summary: Get AFC channel data of a specific AP.

get.description: This API endpoint retrieves list of device's firmware details such as firmware version, upgrade status, recommended version, firmware classification and last upgraded timestamp.

parameters.description: Denotes the maximum number of items returned in the response. Maximum value is 1000.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Specifies the pagination cursor for the next page of resources. The minimum value is 1.
parameters.name: next

get.summary: Get device list with firmware details

get.description: This API provides the insights for each site of a tenant for a given time range.
get.summary: Get the insights for each site of a tenant for a given time range.

get.description: This API provides the trends of visitors and passerby for a tenant across all the sites or for a specific site within the given time range.
get.summary: Get all the trends of a client for a tenant or a site for a given time range.

get.description: Gets a list of scan statuses for AP ranging scans that were triggered on this floor-id.

parameters.description: Site ID where the scan(s) were executed.
parameters.name: site-id

parameters.description: Floor ID where the scan(s) were executed.
parameters.name: floor-id

200.description: List of AP ranging scans matching the floor-id

get.summary: List AP ranging scans

delete.description: Stop a previously created AP ranging scan. Only scans in the PENDING state can successfully be stopped. Once a scan has transitioned to the SCHEDULED or SCANNING states, the user will need to wait for the scan to complete before the network can be restored. Scans in the other states are already stopped, so stopping them will effectively be a no-op.

parameters.description: Site ID where the scan(s) were executed.
parameters.name: site-id

parameters.description: Floor ID where the scan(s) were executed.
parameters.name: floor-id

parameters.description: The Scan ID.
parameters.name: scan-id

get.description: Returns the status of the AP ranging scan identified by the scan-id.

parameters.description: Site ID where the scan(s) were executed.
parameters.name: site-id

parameters.description: Floor ID where the scan(s) were executed.
parameters.name: floor-id

parameters.description: The Scan ID.
parameters.name: scan-id

get.summary: Get scan resource

get.description: This API endpoint retrieves a list of network devices that have been assigned location information (e.g. after being placed on a floorplan and/or automatically determined by the system). Each device in the returned list includes details like serial number, MAC address, IP address, consolidated location, etc. The consolidated location of a device is the best known location of the device (x/y coordinates on the floorplan and/or latitude/longitude geographic coordinates) with the administrator-specified position always taking precedence.  The query parameters allow you to narrow down the results to devices that meet specific criteria, ensuring you receive only the relevant data. Device lists are restricted to a single site represented by the required site-id query parameter.

parameters.description: The ID of the Site for which device location information is requested.
parameters.name: site-id

parameters.description: OData 4.0 filter (limited functionality). Supports only 'and' conjunction ('or' and 'not' are NOT supported). Supports filtering on fields floorId and buildingId only.
parameters.name: filter

parameters.description: Filter for devices with or without location based on this parameter.
parameters.name: with-location

200.description: Returned device list with their consolidated locations.

get.summary: List devices with location information

get.description: This API provides the location details for a specific location resource. The site-id must be provided alongside the location-id to properly fetch the location data. Each unique location-id represents a change in device location, for example if an AP was moved from one floorplan to another a new location resource with a unique location-id will be created.

parameters.description: ID of the Site for which device location information is requested.
parameters.name: site-id

parameters.description: The ID of the location resource to be fetched.
parameters.name: location-id

get.summary: Get location resource

delete.description:
Remove the adminSpecifiedLocation coordinates of a device. The device must be identified by the site-id and serial-number.
If the AP has a built-in GPS receiver and a GPS position is known, then the consolidated device location will fall back to the last known GPS position.

parameters.description: The ID of the Site for which device location information should be removed.
parameters.name: site-id

parameters.description: The device serial for which the location information is being removed.
parameters.name: serial-number

202.description: Device coordinates delete accepted. Location will be updated shortly in the database.

get.description: Return the location information for a device identified by the required parameters site-id and serial-number. The device must exist in the system and have associated location data.

parameters.description: The ID of the Site for which device location information is requested.
parameters.name: site-id

parameters.description: The device serial for which the location information is being requested.
parameters.name: serial-number

200.description:
Object containing the device's geographic coordinates matching the request criteria. 
If no device with the requested information is found, then an empty list is returned.

get.summary: Get device location details

post.description:
This API can set or update the adminSpecifiedLocation of an AP. The device to update must be identified by both the site-id and serial-number and MUST exist in the system to set its location. The adminSpecifiedLocation of a device will always override the latest consolidatedLocation.
If the `center` is provided in the request body, these coordinates will be programmed into the AP to support FTM based client location services (if enabled).

parameters.description: The ID of the Site for which device location information is being set.
parameters.name: site-id

parameters.description: The device serial for which the location information is being requested.
parameters.name: serial-number

schema.description: Administratively determined device geo and relative location.

202.description: Device coordinates update accepted. Location will be updated shortly in the database.

get.description: This API endpoint retrieves a list of Webhooks and the details associated to the receiver such as the endpointURL and the authMechanism along with createdAt and updatedAt timestamps.
get.summary: List all Webhooks

post.description: This API endpoint creates a new Webhook using details of the receiver such as the endpoint URL and the authMechanism. Auth mechanism can be either API key or OIDC. Use dropdown beside input in body parameters to change authMechanism and provide respective parameters for the authentication type.

requestBody.description: Request body for creating a new Webhook.

delete.description: This API endpoint deletes an existing Webhook.

get.description: This API endpoint retrieves Webhook details for the specified Webhook ID such as the endpointURL and the authMechanism along with createdAt and updatedAt timestamps.
get.summary: Get a Webhook details by ID

patch.description: This API endpoint modifies an existing Webhook. One or multiple parameters in the body can be used to update the Webhook. Example - updating the endpoint URL for a existing Webhook. To only change name and endpoint parameters, select Payload in dropdown. To change Auth mechanism, select "Authentication Update" in the dropdown and select authentication type.

requestBody.description: Request body for patching an existing Webhook.

put.description: This API endpoint modifies an existing Webhook. All the request body parameters should be provided to update the Webhook. Auth mechanism can be either API key or OIDC. Use dropdown beside input in body parameters to change authMechanism and provide respective parameters for the authentication type.

requestBody.description: Request body for updating an existing Webhook.

post.description: This API endpoint rotates the HMAC key for an existing Webhook.

parameters.description: Unique identifier for the webhook. This can be obtained from the List Webhooks API.
parameters.name: id

post.summary: Rotate HMAC key for a Webhook by ID

get.description: This API provides Wi-Fi client locations using a variety of query options. Please use either `latest-location-per-client` or `latest-connected-client-mac` query parameters. For details please refer to descriptions of corresponding parameters.

parameters.description:
This is required parameter if latest-location-per-client parameter is used. OData Version 4.0 filter string (limited functionality). Supports only 'and' conjunction ('or' and 'not' are NOT supported). Supported fields and operators are in the below given table.

| Field | Operators |
| --------- | -------- |
| `siteId` (mandatory) | `eq` and `in` |
| `buildingId` | `eq` and `in` |
| `floorId` | `eq` and `in` |
| `associated` | `eq` |
| `connected` | `eq` |

**The operators are implemented as follows:**

* `eq`: Only values exactly matching the given value are returned.

* `in`: Only values present in the given list are returned.

 Note: Provide boolean values (true/false) in lowercase.
parameters.example: siteId eq '1033566193' and buildingId in ('buildingId1','buildingId2') and floorId in ('defaultFloorId3', 'defaultFloorId4') and associated eq true and connected eq false
parameters.name: filter

parameters.description: Retrieve data starting at the specified timestamp. Provided in RFC 3339 format. Example: `2023-01-01T23:10:41.123Z`. If no timestamp is provided, all data will be considered.
parameters.name: start-at

parameters.description: Denotes the maximum number of clients returned in the response. (Default: 100)
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Specifies the zero-based resource offset to start the page from. Default: 0
parameters.name: offset

parameters.description: Provides a list of latest client locations. Only the value of 1 is supported for the moment. It is mutually exclusive from parameter latest-connected-client-mac.
parameters.name: latest-location-per-client

parameters.description: Provides the latest location information for a specific client that is connected and associated. Do not provide filter, offset and limit when using this query parameter. It is mutually exclusive from parameter latest-location-per-client.
parameters.name: latest-connected-client-mac

get.summary: Get locations of Wi-Fi clients

tags.description: APIs that allow for interacting with Location Service offerings.
tags.name: Location

tags.description: Location Analytics APIs to get the client related analytics describing how the client is classified.
tags.name: Location Analytics
